title: CallLint Evidence Provider Envelope
description: A normalized envelope (calllint.evidence-provider.v0) for a third-party scanner's report (e.g. SkillSpector, OSV, Semgrep). CallLint records the external evidence with provenance — provider, version, scan mode, coverage, completeness, raw-report digest — WITHOUT re-scoring or renaming the provider's own findings/verdict. An external SAFE never upgrades a CallLint verdict; a degraded/failed/malformed scan fails closed (never reads as a pass). Evidence is aggregated, never impersonated. See ADR 0034 and docs/new7-packet-a-evidence.md.

schema_version.description: Envelope schema identity. Distinct from calllint.report.v0 / calllint.receipt.v0.

providerVersion.description: Pinned provider version. Prefer "git:<40-hex-commit>" for tools with no formal release (SkillSpector). "unknown" is allowed but MUST coincide with completeness != complete and a degradedReasons entry.

artifactDigest.description: Digest of the artifact that was scanned (the skill/config/package), if known. sha256:<64hex>.

scanMode.description: How the external scan ran. An LLM-assisted scan is decision-relevant downstream.

completeness.description: How complete the external scan was. Anything other than "complete" MUST NOT read as a pass; "failed" is fail-closed.

findings.description: Provider-native findings, kept verbatim. CallLint never remaps providerSeverity into its own severity hierarchy, nor renames providerRuleId.

providerRuleId.description: The provider's own rule/check id, e.g. "SS-EXFIL-001". Never rewritten into a CallLint rule id.

providerSeverity.description: The provider's own severity string, verbatim (not mapped to CallLint's S0-S5 / verdicts).

message.description: Optional human-readable finding message from the provider.

rawReportDigest.description: sha256 of the stable-stringified raw external report. Preserves provenance; the raw report is never paraphrased away.

startedAt.description: Optional ISO-8601 UTC start time of the external scan.

finishedAt.description: Optional ISO-8601 UTC finish time of the external scan.

degradedReasons.description: Why the scan is not fully complete/trustworthy (unpinned version, parse loss, tool error, offline). Non-empty REQUIRES completeness in {partial, degraded, failed}.
